div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in Switzerland (Wilson), at Geneva
div.p: 140. Your 235, April 12, 6 p.m. The Brazilian Ambassador advised the Department today, by instruction of his Government, that the Leticia negotiations at Rio had suddenly taken a very favorable turn, and that the Brazilian Government feared that intervention by the League in the question at this moment might endanger a successful accord between Colombia and Peru. The Brazilian Government requested that the United States Government support the suggestion it had made at Geneva that for the reason above expressed, the League refrain for the time being from any move in the matter. He was advised that this Government, of course, could officially take no action in accordance with the request made, but that the request of the Brazilian Government and the information given would be transmitted to you for such informal and discreet use thereof as you might deem it possible to make.
